name: Chat with GitHub OpenAPI Specification using RAG (Pinecone and OpenAI)
description: Automated workflow: Chat with GitHub OpenAPI Specification using RAG (Pinecone and OpenAI). This workflow integrates 13 different services: stickyNote, httpRequest, vectorStorePinecone, textSplitterRecursiveCharacterTextSplitter, agent. It contains 23 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.

nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
## Indexing content in the vector database
This part of the workflow is responsible for extracting content, generating embeddings and sending them to the Pinecone vector store.

It requests the OpenAPI specifications from GitHub using a HTTP request. Then, it splits the file in chunks, generating embeddings for each chunk using OpenAI, and saving them in Pinecone vector DB.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
## Querying and response generation 

This part of the workflow is responsible for the chat interface, querying the vector store and generating relevant responses.

It uses OpenAI GPT 4o-mini to generate responses.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
## RAG workflow in n8n

This is an example of how to use RAG techniques to create a chatbot with n8n. It is an API documentation chatbot that can answer questions about the GitHub API. It uses OpenAI for generating embeddings, the gpt-4o-mini LLM for generating responses and Pinecone as a vector database.

### Before using this template
* create OpenAI and Pinecone accounts
* obtain API keys OpenAI and Pinecone 
* configure credentials in n8n for both
* ensure you have a Pinecone index named "n8n-demo" or adjust the workflow accordingly.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

options.systemMessage: You are a helpful assistant providing information about the GitHub API and how to use it based on the OpenAPI V3 specifications.

parameters.content:
## Indexing content in the vector database
This part of the workflow is responsible for extracting content, generating embeddings and sending them to the Pinecone vector store.

It requests the OpenAPI specifications from GitHub using a HTTP request. Then, it splits the file in chunks, generating embeddings for each chunk using OpenAI, and saving them in Pinecone vector DB.

## Querying and response generation 

This part of the workflow is responsible for the chat interface, querying the vector store and generating relevant responses.

It uses OpenAI GPT 4o-mini to generate responses.

## RAG workflow in n8n

This is an example of how to use RAG techniques to create a chatbot with n8n. It is an API documentation chatbot that can answer questions about the GitHub API. It uses OpenAI for generating embeddings, the gpt-4o-mini LLM for generating responses and Pinecone as a vector database.

### Before using this template
* create OpenAI and Pinecone accounts
* obtain API keys OpenAI and Pinecone 
* configure credentials in n8n for both
* ensure you have a Pinecone index named "n8n-demo" or adjust the workflow accordingly.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "When clicking ‘Test workflow’".
Open the "HTTP Request" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Pinecone Vector Store" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.

Open the "Vector Store Tool" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OpenAI Chat Model1" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.

No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
